title: Machine learning for ECG diagnosis and risk stratification of occlusion myocardial infarction

template.name: Diagnostic performance
template.instructions: Extract reported model discrimination and diagnostic accuracy.

outcomes.name: Discriminative Performance
outcomes.description: Discrimination and diagnostic accuracy of the model versus comparators.

source: Machine learning for ECG diagnosis of occlusion myocardial infarction. In the held-out test set, the random forest (RF) model achieved an area under the receiver operating characteristic (AUROC) of 0.91 (95% CI, 0.87–0.96), outperforming practicing clinicians (AUROC 0.79, 95% CI 0.73–0.86) and the commercial ECG system (AUROC 0.78, 95% CI 0.70–0.85). At the optimal operating threshold, sensitivity was 0.88 and specificity was 0.83, with a positive predictive value (PPV) of 0.71 and a negative predictive value (NPV) of 0.94.
